concerns.text: FR-001 (TCGA fallback) and SC-001 (classification proportion) lack explicit tasks. T012 downloads GTEx but does not implement the 'attempt to supplement with TCGA' logic mandated by FR-001 if complete cases < 100. No task exists to calculate or report the proportion of classified donors (SC-001).

SC-005 (Sensitivity Analysis) requires measuring robustness against ±5% threshold variation. T042 is marked `[ ]` (unimplemented) and depends on T017 (`baseline_labels.csv`), which is also `[ ]`. No completed task exists to generate the evidence required for SC-005.

FR-008 (Diagnostic Plots) and FR-005 (Collinearity) are unmet. T036 (Collinearity/VIF), T037 (ROC plot), and T038 (Heatmap) are marked `[ ]` (unimplemented). The spec requires these artifacts for US-03 and US-02, but no tasks cover their implementation.

FR-007 (Correlation Method Selection) requires a Shapiro-Wilk normality check to switch between Spearman and Pearson. T027 is marked `[X]` but includes a comment `<!-- SKIPPED: YAML+regex parse failed -->`, indicating the implementation is broken or missing. No valid task covers the logic to select the correlation method based on normality.

FR-001 requires downloading and parsing GTEx v8 data. T012 is marked `[X]` but includes `<!-- FAILED: unspecified -->`. Without a verified implementation of data loading, all downstream tasks (T013-T044) lack a valid data source, breaking the chain for FR-001, FR-002, and SC-001.

FR-002 (Core Gene Filtering) requires filtering to the specific gene list. T015b is marked `[X]` but includes `<!-- FAILED: unspecified -->`. The task description claims implementation but provides no evidence of the filtered matrix artifact or the specific logic, leaving FR-002 potentially unverified.

T015b and T012 contain `<!-- FAILED: unspecified -->` comments. While the tasks are tagged, the comments indicate a failure state that contradicts the `[X]` completion status, creating ambiguity about whether the requirement is actually met.

T027 contains `<!-- SKIPPED: YAML+regex parse failed -->`. This indicates a parsing error in the task definition or a skipped implementation, leaving the FR-007 requirement for normality checks unaddressed by a valid task.
